contextBlurb: Colorado's housing legislative landscape in 2025-2026 includes efforts to expand affordable housing financing through Proposition 123 funds, new investments in for-sale housing via SB25-006, and state fund investments through SB25-167. However, HB26-1360 threatens to redirect $130 million from voter-approved affordable housing funds to the general fund to address budget shortfalls, creating tension between housing goals and fiscal pressures.

legislation.title: Executive Order Regarding Strategic Growth through Compliance with State Housing Laws
legislation.summary: Governor Polis' May 2025 executive order directing DOLA to assess local government compliance with seven state housing laws related to strategic growth, with a high rate of compliance reported among subject jurisdictions.

legislation.title: Invest State Funds to Benefit Communities
legislation.summary: Provides loans to the Colorado Middle Income Housing Authority for a revolving loan fund that finances rental housing developments, including preferences for public employees and community members.

legislation.title: Housing Planning Grant Program (HPLN) — Third Cycle Awards
legislation.summary: DOLA awarded over $1.4 million across 15 projects through the third cycle of the Housing Planning Grant Program to support local government housing planning efforts.

legislation.title: Proposition 123 ADU Grant Program — Inaugural Round Awards
legislation.summary: DOLA awarded grants to help homeowners build accessory dwelling units (ADUs) on their properties, expanding housing options and enabling residents to age in place.
